content.body: A bearing is a way of describing direction using angles measured clockwise from North. Bearings are always written as 3 digits (e.g. 045°, 090°, 270°).
Cardinal directions: N=000°, E=090°, S=180°, W=270°

content.title: Reading Bearings
content.body:
A bearing of 060° means 60° clockwise from North — this is between North and East (NE direction).
A bearing of 225° is between South and West (SW direction).

content.title: Back Bearing
content.body: If town B is on a bearing of 075° from town A, then town A is on a bearing of 075° + 180° = 255° from town B.

questions.question: Bearings are always measured from:
questions.explanation: By convention, all bearings start from North and are measured clockwise.

questions.question: The bearing of East is:
questions.explanation: East is exactly 90° clockwise from North.

questions.question: A bearing of 180° points in which direction?
questions.explanation: 180° clockwise from North points due South.

questions.question: How many digits are used to write a bearing?
NE direction as a bearing is:
questions.explanation: NE is exactly halfway between N(000°) and E(090°) = 045°

questions.question: The back bearing of 110° is:
A bearing of 315° corresponds to which compass direction?
Town Y is on bearing 240° from town X. Bearing of X from Y is:
Which bearing is in the SE quadrant?
questions.explanation: SE quadrant is between 090° and 180°. 125° falls there.

questions.question: A ship travels on bearing 270°. Which direction is it heading?
SW bearing is:
questions.explanation: SW = 225°, exactly halfway between S(180°) and W(270°).

questions.question: A bearing of 000° is:
Back bearing of 350° is:
A plane flies from A on bearing 060° for 100km to B. Direction from A to B is:
questions.explanation: 060° is in the NE quadrant (between N=000° and E=090°)

questions.question: Bearing SE = ?
questions.explanation: SE = 135°, halfway between E(090°) and S(180°).

questions.question: Which statement about bearings is TRUE?
questions.explanation: Even 5° is written 005°. This is standard notation.

questions.question: A hiker walks on bearing 090°. They turn to walk on back bearing. New bearing is:
If you face North then turn 135° clockwise, your bearing is:
A bearing between 180° and 270° is in which quadrant?
questions.explanation: 180°=S, 270°=W, so between them is SW quadrant.

questions.question: Back bearing of 045° is:

content.body: A scale drawing represents real objects at a reduced (or enlarged) size. The scale tells us the ratio between the drawing and reality.

Scale = Drawing length : Real length
Example: 1:50,000 means 1cm on map = 50,000cm in reality

Real distance = Drawing distance × Scale factor
Drawing distance = Real distance ÷ Scale factor

content.title: Using a Map Scale
content.body:
A map has scale 1:200,000.
Two towns are 3.5cm apart on the map.
Real distance = 3.5 × 200,000 = 700,000cm = 7km

content.title: Drawing to Scale
content.body:
A room is 6m long. Scale = 1:100.
Drawing length = 6m ÷ 100 = 0.06m = 6cm
Scale drawings are used in maps, architectural plans, engineering drawings. Always state the scale clearly. Scales like 1:50 or 1cm = 50m are equivalent ways to express the same ratio.

questions.question: A scale of 1:100 means:
questions.explanation: 1:100 means every 1cm on drawing = 100cm in real life.

questions.explanation: Smaller scale factor = larger drawing relative to reality = more detail.

questions.explanation: Without stating the scale, a drawing cannot be used to find real distances.

questions.answer: Drawing is same size as real object
questions.explanation: 1:1 = true size drawing, used for very small objects.

questions.explanation: Small-scale numbers (1:1,000) give most detail — used for town/building plans.

questions.explanation: Maps use scale to show distance and bearings to show direction — both essential for navigation.
